User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the Asnap social media application, storing profile information and relationships with other users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

username.description: The unique display name chosen by the user.

email.description: The user's email address, used for account recovery and notifications.

bio.description: A short biographical description provided by the user for their profile.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user's profile was last updated.

Post.title: Post
Post.description: Represents an ephemeral media post (image or video) shared by a user, designed to disappear after 48 hours.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Post entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created the post. (Relationship: User 1:N Post)

mediaUrl.description: URL to the image or video content of the post.

caption.description: The text description accompanying the post.

hashtags.description: An array of hashtags associated with the post.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the post was created.

expiresAt.description: Timestamp when the post is scheduled to be automatically deleted (48 hours after creation).

likeCount.description: The total number of likes this post has received. (Denormalized count)

commentCount.description: The total number of comments this post has received. (Denormalized count)

viewCount.description: The total number of times the post has been viewed.

Story.title: Story
Story.description: Represents a short-lived photo or video story shared by a user, disappearing after 24 hours.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Story entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created the story. (Relationship: User 1:N Story)

mediaUrl.description: URL to the image or video content of the story.

caption.description: Optional text overlay on the story.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the story was created.

expiresAt.description: Timestamp when the story is scheduled to be automatically deleted (24 hours after creation).

Comment.title: Comment
Comment.description: Represents a user's comment on a post.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Comment entity.

postId.description: Reference to the Post this comment belongs to. (Relationship: Post 1:N Comment)

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the comment. (Relationship: User 1:N Comment)

content.description: The textual content of the comment.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the comment was created.

Like.title: Like
Like.description: Represents a user's 'like' reaction to a post.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Like entity.

postId.description: Reference to the Post that was liked. (Relationship: Post 1:N Like)

userId.description: Reference to the User who liked the post. (Relationship: User 1:N Like)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the like was recorded.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents an in-app notification for a user, triggered by events like follows, likes, or comments.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

recipientId.description: The ID of the user who receives the notification.

senderId.description: The ID of the user who triggered the notification (e.g., the follower).

read.description: Indicates whether the notification has been read by the recipient.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the notification was created.

postId.description: Optional ID of the post related to the notification (for likes and comments).

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The `id` field within the document must match the `userId` in the path and `request.auth.uid`. Can be read by anyone for public profile data, but write access is restricted to the owner.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores notifications for a specific user. Read access is restricted to the owner (`userId`). Write access for creation is granted to the sender.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user receiving the notification.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the notification.

definition.description: Represents a follower relationship, allowing efficient `exists()` checks to determine if `followerUserId` follows `followedUserId`. Used for authorization independence in read rules for posts/stories. Document content can be minimal (e.g., `{ createdAt: Timestamp }`).

params.name: followedUserId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user being followed.

params.name: followerUserId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is following.

definition.description: Represents a 'following' relationship, allowing a user to efficiently query who they follow. Document content can be minimal (e.g., `{ createdAt: Timestamp }`). This is a symmetrical relationship to `user_followers` for easier lookup.

params.name: followerUserId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is doing the following.

params.name: followedUserId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user being followed.

definition.description: Stores individual ephemeral posts created by a user. The `userId` field within the document must match `postOwnerId` in the path. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence. Accessible by the post owner and their followers.

params.name: postOwnerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this post.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier of the post.

definition.description: Stores individual ephemeral stories created by a user. The `userId` field within the document must match `storyOwnerId` in the path. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence. Accessible by the story owner and their followers.

params.name: storyOwnerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this story.

params.name: storyId
params.description: The unique identifier of the story.

definition.description: Stores comments for a specific post. The `userId` field within the document (commenter ID) must match `request.auth.uid` for write/delete access. Includes denormalized `postId` and `userId` for authorization independence. Access is granted if `request.auth.uid` is the `postOwnerId`, `commenterUserId`, or follows `postOwnerId`.

params.name: postOwnerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent post.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent post.

params.name: commentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the comment.

definition.description: Stores 'likes' for a specific post. The `userId` field within the document (liker ID) must match `request.auth.uid` for write/delete access. It is recommended that `likeId` is set to `request.auth.uid` to ensure uniqueness per user per post. Includes denormalized `postId` and `userId` for authorization independence. Access is granted if `request.auth.uid` is the `postOwnerId`, `likerUserId`, or follows `postOwnerId`.

params.name: postOwnerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent post.

params.name: postId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent post.

params.name: likeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the like, recommended to be the `userId` of the liker.

definition.description: A denormalized feed of posts for each user. Each document contains a snapshot of a post from a followed user, including `originalPostId` and `originalPostOwnerId`. This collection is read-only by the `feedViewerId` and populated by a backend process to ensure QAP compliance for the personalized content feed.

params.name: feedViewerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user to whom this feed belongs.

params.name: feedPostId
params.description: The unique identifier of the post item within the feed (could be the `originalPostId`).

definition.description: A denormalized feed of stories for each user. Each document contains a snapshot of a story from a followed user, including `originalStoryId` and `originalStoryOwnerId`. This collection is read-only by the `storyFeedViewerId` and populated by a backend process to ensure QAP compliance for interactive story sharing.

params.name: storyFeedViewerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user to whom this story feed belongs.

params.name: feedStoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the story item within the feed (could be the `originalStoryId`).

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for Asnap prioritizes Authorization Independence and QAP-compliant list operations by employing structural segregation, path-based ownership, and strategic denormalization. Each core entity has a dedicated collection, and subcollections are used to enforce natural hierarchies and ownership.

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **Users:** The `/users/{userId}` path ensures that a user's profile data (`userId`) directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid` for write operations, making authorization self-contained for the user's own profile.
2.  **Follower/Following Relationships:** Instead of relying on potentially large arrays within the `User` document, dedicated subcollections `/user_followers/{followedUserId}/followers/{followerUserId}` and `/user_following/{followerUserId}/following/{followedUserId}` are used. This allows for efficient `exists()` checks in security rules to determine if one user follows another, completely avoiding `get()` calls to other documents for this common authorization check. The `followedUserId` and `followerUserId` are denormalized into the path and implicitly into the document structure, making authorization of these records independent.
3.  **Posts and Stories:** Documents in `/users/{postOwnerId}/posts/{postId}` and `/users/{storyOwnerId}/stories/{storyId}` include `userId` (owner) as a denormalized field. For writes, `request.auth.uid` must match both the path parameter (`postOwnerId` or `storyOwnerId`) and the denormalized `userId` field, ensuring strict ownership without needing to `get()` a parent document. For reads, the `exists()` check on the `/user_followers` collection is used, which does not break atomic operations as per the mandate.
4.  **Comments and Likes:** These are nested under their respective posts: `/users/{postOwnerId}/posts/{postId}/comments/{commentId}` and `/users/{postOwnerId}/posts/{postId}/likes/{likeId}`. Each `Comment` document includes the `userId` of the commenter, and each `Like` document includes the `userId` of the liker (recommended `likeId` is `request.auth.uid`). This denormalizes the commenter/liker ownership to the document itself. The `postOwnerId` and `postId` are available directly in the path. For writes, `request.auth.uid` must match the denormalized `userId`. The existence of the parent post can be checked using `exists()` without violating atomicity for the comment/like operation itself.
5.  **User Feeds:** The core solution for personalized feeds is total denormalization into `/user_feeds/{feedViewerId}/feed_posts/{feedPostId}` and `/user_story_feeds/{storyFeedViewerId}/feed_stories/{feedStoryId}`. Each document in these collections contains a full snapshot of the original post/story content required for display, along with `originalPostId` and `originalPostOwnerId`. The authorization for reading these documents is simply `request.auth.uid == feedViewerId`, which is entirely self-contained and requires no `get()` calls.

**QAP (Rules are not Filters) Support:**
1.  **User Profiles:** `/users/{userId}` allows for direct `get` requests for specific user profiles and `list` requests across the collection for public profile information (e.g., username search), relying on client-side queries and server-side rules based on available fields.
2.  **Personalized Content Feeds:** The `/user_feeds/{feedViewerId}/feed_posts` and `/user_story_feeds/{storyFeedViewerId}/feed_stories` collections are explicitly designed for QAPs. Users fetch their own feed by querying `db.collection('user_feeds').doc(request.auth.uid).collection('feed_posts')`. Security rules can simply enforce `allow read: if feedViewerId == request.auth.uid`. This allows for efficient, paginated list operations without the need for complex, unindexable filters within security rules, as the feed is pre-computed and delivered.
3.  **Owner-Specific Content:** Paths like `/users/{postOwnerId}/posts` or `/users/{storyOwnerId}/stories` allow `list` operations to retrieve all posts/stories by a specific user. The security rules ensure that `request.auth.uid` matches `postOwnerId` or `storyOwnerId` for write/delete, and allows read access if `request.auth.uid` is the owner or follows the owner using the efficient `exists()` check.
4.  **Comments and Likes:** `list` operations for comments and likes of a specific post (`/users/{postOwnerId}/posts/{postId}/comments` or `/users/{postOwnerId}/posts/{postId}/likes`) are QAP-compliant. The security rules ensure that the user has appropriate access (owner, commenter/liker, or follower of post owner) to the parent post before allowing listing of its subcollections. This avoids filtering by complex criteria in rules.
